title: 埃及十字架之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\27-埃及十字架之谜-埃勒里奎因.txt
summary:
After Jonah Lincoln retrieves a large blue atlas from the study, Ellery Queen examines its maps and indexes to identify a pattern. Ellery observes that several individuals connected to the case have used pseudonyms derived from geographical locations: Thomas Braid is a Romanian city, Stephen Megalos is a Greek city, and the deceased Andrew Van corresponds to a city and lake in Armenia. However, Ellery notes that the name "Crocetta" does not correspond to any location in Europe or the Near East, suggesting it is a real name rather than a pseudonym. Professor Yardley concludes that Crocetta is likely the true identity of one of the men using a fake name, which may provide a key to understanding the "hieroglyphic 'T'."

The group moves to the right wing of the house to visit Thomas Braid's study, where they are served lunch by the housekeeper, Mrs. Baxter. After Professor Adler departs for his own lunch, Ellery, Escham, and Vawn enter the study at two o'clock. The room contains built-in bookshelves, a small piano in an alcove, and a desk with nearly full red and black ink bottles. Escham notes that a previous examination of the desk with a magnifying glass yielded no valuable files. Since the murder occurred in the gazebo, Escham concludes that the only remaining element in the room worth studying is the chess set.
